summary: Tested Chat and Meeting features. Backend APIs work correctly (100% pass). Socket.io through external ingress has connection issues causing real-time messaging failures - messages sent via socket are NOT persisted to DB. Internal socket (localhost:8001) works perfectly. Fixed 2 bugs: profile API id issue and meetingRooms scope issue.

critical.issue: Socket.io connections through Kubernetes ingress fail with 'server error' and 'parser error'. Messages sent via socket from frontend are NOT saved to database. WebSocket upgrade fails through ingress.
critical.root_cause: Kubernetes ingress not properly configured for socket.io/websocket upgrade
critical.evidence: Direct socket connection to localhost:8001 works perfectly and saves messages. External URL connection fails.

integration_issues.issue: Messages appear in UI (optimistic update) but are NOT saved to database because socket.io connection through external URL is broken
Cannot fully test multi-user meeting connection due to socket.io infrastructure issue

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix Socket.io routing through Kubernetes ingress - current config does not properly handle websocket upgrade. Consider adding sticky sessions or websocket-specific annotations to ingress.
Alternative: If ingress cannot be fixed, add a fallback to send messages via REST API when socket fails
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: /app/backend/routes/profile.js - GET /me endpoint now returns 'id' instead of '_id' for consistency with login response
FIXED: /app/backend/server.js - meetingRooms Map was incorrectly scoped inside io.on('connection') handler. Each connection created its own empty Map. Moved to global scope.
Backend socket message handling code is correct - verified by direct localhost connection test

success_rate.frontend: Partial - UI loads but socket-dependent features broken

test_credentials.note: Users are connected to each other for testing

seed_data_creation: Created two test users (testa@chattest.com, testb@chattest.com) and connected them. Sent test messages via REST API.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Socket.io works internally (localhost:8001) but fails through external URL. To test real-time features properly, either fix ingress configuration OR implement REST API fallback for messaging. Backend message persistence via REST API is confirmed working. Profile /me endpoint now returns 'id' field. meetingRooms Map is now properly shared globally.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: Socket.io connections through the Kubernetes ingress receive 'server error' with 'parser error' code. The ingress is not properly handling the socket.io polling transport. WebSocket upgrade is also failing ('WebSocket is closed before the connection is established').
reproduction_steps: 2. Observe 'server error' with 'parser error' on connection
3. Same client connecting to http://localhost:8001 works perfectly
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: Options: (1) Configure ingress with sticky sessions and websocket support annotations, (2) Add REST API fallback for message sending when socket fails, (3) Use a separate subdomain for socket.io that bypasses ingress issues

bugs_fixed_this_iteration.issue: GET /me returned raw MongoDB document with _id, frontend expected 'id'
meetingRooms Map defined inside io.on('connection') - each user got their own empty Map
bugs_fixed_this_iteration.fix: Moved meetingRooms declaration to module scope before io.on('connection')
